chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. NO. D-5569 / 2025 (Imran Vs. Federation of Pakistan & Others) ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE 1) For orders on CMA No. 23216/2025. 2) For orders on office objection No. 1 to 3. 3) For orders on CMA No. 23217/2025. 4) For orders on CMA No. 23218/2025. 5) For hearing of main case. 17.11.2025. Mr. Sardar Muhammad Ishaque, Advocate for Petitioner. ___________________ 1) Urgency granted. 3) Exemption granted. 2, 4 & 5) The petitioner seeks directions for implementing an order of implementation passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal dated 13.01.2025. Learned counsel submits that despite such implementation order, the department has not released the petitioner’s vehicle. The implementation order of the Tribunal dated 13.01.2025 recites that if the same is not implemented, proceedings under section 188 PPC and 194(C) sub-section 8 of the Customs Act, 1969 shall be initiated against the relevant officers of the Customs. It appears that the Tribunal did not take further action inasmuch as an SCRA was pending at that time. Learned counsel acknowledges that the SCRA has also been dismissed, he does not demonstrate that the petitioner moved any further application to the Tribunal thereafter, for following up the implementation order dated 13.01.2025. As already noted in that order, the Tribunal has ample power to enforce its own order. Therefore, petition is misconceived and dismissed. J U D G E J U D G E Arshad/
